project_specification.overview: A lightweight web application for small teams to create projects, assign tasks, track progress, and leave comments. Users authenticate with email and password and can only see their own team's data. Designed to be deployed as a single-server app with a PostgreSQL database and a React front end served by a FastAPI backend.
project_specification.target_audience: Small development teams (2–15 people) who want a self-hosted task tracker without the overhead of large project management tools.

frontend.state_management: TanStack Query (server state); React useState/useReducer (local state)

database.dev: SQLite (via DATABASE_URL env toggle for local development)

project_specification.prerequisites: - Node.js 20+ and npm installed - Python 3.12+ with uv installed - PostgreSQL 16 running (or Docker) - Environment variables: DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, FRONTEND_URL - Docker and docker-compose (optional, for containerised dev)

core_features.category: - User can register with email and display name via the /register page - System validates email format and rejects duplicates with a 409 Conflict error - System validates password: minimum 8 characters, at least one number - System hashes passwords with bcrypt before storing in the database - System returns 201 with the user profile object on successful registration - User can log in with email and password via the /login page - System returns a JWT access_token (15 min expiry) and refresh_token (7 day expiry) on successful login - System returns 401 with "Invalid credentials" on failed login (no per-field detail) - System rate-limits login attempts to 5 per minute per IP address, returning 429 on excess - User can log out; system invalidates the refresh_token in the database - System silently refreshes the access_token using the refresh_token when it expires - System returns 401 on all protected endpoints when the token is missing, malformed, or expired - User can view their profile: display name, email, and account creation date - User can update their display name from the profile settings page - User can change their password by providing the current password and a new password - System rejects a password change if the current password is incorrect with a 400 error

- User can create a project with a name and optional description - System returns 201 with the new project object on successful creation - User can view a list of all projects they belong to on the /projects page - User can click a project to open its task board - User can edit a project's name or description from the project settings page - User can delete a project; all associated tasks and comments are deleted (cascade) - System returns 403 if a user tries to access a project they are not a member of - Project creator is automatically added as the project owner - Owner can invite a user to a project by entering their email address - System sends an email invitation with a join link (valid for 48 hours) - Invited user can accept the invitation and is added to the project as a member - Owner can remove a member from a project - Project member list shows each member's display name, email, and join date

- User can create a task with a title, optional description, and optional due date - System returns 201 with the new task object on successful creation - Tasks belong to a project and are only visible to project members - Task status can be one of: todo, in_progress, done (defaults to todo) - User can update a task's title, description, due date, or status - User can assign a task to any project member via a dropdown - User can unassign a task by clearing the assignee field - User can set a task priority: low, medium, high (defaults to medium) - User can delete a task; all associated comments are deleted (cascade) - System returns 404 with a descriptive message when a task ID does not exist - System returns 403 if a non-member tries to access or modify a task - Task list supports filtering by status, assignee, and priority - Task list supports sorting by created date, due date, and priority - Overdue tasks (past due date with status not done) are highlighted in red

- User can add a comment to any task they can view - Comment body supports plain text up to 4000 characters - System returns 201 with the new comment object on successful creation - Comments are displayed in chronological order below the task description - User can edit their own comment body within 10 minutes of posting - System returns 403 if a user tries to edit another user's comment - User can delete their own comment at any time; project owners can delete any comment - Deleted comments are replaced with "Comment deleted" placeholder text (soft delete) - System shows commenter display name, avatar initial, and relative timestamp (e.g., "2 hours ago")

- App is fully responsive on mobile (375px+), tablet (768px+), and desktop (1280px+) - Navigation sidebar is visible on desktop; collapses to a hamburger menu on mobile - All list and detail pages use skeleton screen loading states (not spinners) - All async actions (create, update, delete) show an inline loading indicator on the button - All form validation errors appear inline below the relevant field (not just as toasts) - App shows success/error toast notifications in the top-right corner (auto-dismiss after 4 seconds) - All destructive actions (delete project, delete task) require a confirmation dialog - App supports dark mode via system preference with a manual toggle in the header - Dark mode preference is persisted in localStorage and applied on page load - Active filters and sort state are preserved in URL query parameters (shareable, bookmarkable) - App uses consistent iconography from the lucide-react icon library - All primary buttons have hover, active, and disabled states - App shows a 404 page for unknown routes with a link back to the dashboard

- API returns consistent JSON envelope: { "data": ..., "error": null, "status": 200 } - API returns { "data": null, "error": { "message": "...", "code": "..." }, "status": 4xx } on errors - API validates all request bodies with Pydantic v2 and returns 400 with field-level errors - API returns 401 for all requests with a missing, malformed, or expired JWT - API returns 403 when an authenticated user attempts to access another user's resource - API returns 404 with a descriptive message when a resource is not found - API returns 500 with a unique error_id for unexpected server errors (error_id logged server-side) - API enables CORS for the FRONTEND_URL environment variable - API rate-limits all endpoints to 200 requests per minute per user - API logs all requests: timestamp, user_id, method, path, response status, and latency - API documents all endpoints via OpenAPI 3.0 at /docs (Swagger UI) - API returns paginated list responses as: { "items": [...], "total": N, "page": 1, "per_page": 25, "has_next": true }

table.name: users
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL
password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
display_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL
created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()

table.name: refresh_tokens
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
token_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE
expires_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL
created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()

table.name: projects
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
description TEXT
owner_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()

table.name: project_members
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
joined_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
UNIQUE(project_id, user_id)

table.name: tasks
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
title VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL
description TEXT
status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'todo' CHECK (status IN ('todo', 'in_progress', 'done'))
priority VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium' CHECK (priority IN ('low', 'medium', 'high'))
assignee_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
due_date DATE
created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id)
created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
INDEX idx_tasks_project_status ON tasks(project_id, status)

table.name: comments
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
task_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
author_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
body TEXT NOT NULL
deleted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE
created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()

api_endpoints_summary.domain: POST /api/auth/register - Register new user account POST /api/auth/login - Log in and receive JWT tokens POST /api/auth/logout - Log out and invalidate refresh token POST /api/auth/refresh - Refresh access token using refresh token
GET /api/users/me - Get current user profile PATCH /api/users/me - Update display name POST /api/users/me/password - Change password

GET /api/projects - List all projects the user is a member of POST /api/projects - Create a new project GET /api/projects/{id} - Get project details and member list PATCH /api/projects/{id} - Update project name or description DELETE /api/projects/{id} - Delete project and all associated data POST /api/projects/{id}/invites - Invite a user by email DELETE /api/projects/{id}/members/{user_id} - Remove a member

GET /api/projects/{id}/tasks - List tasks (supports filter, sort, pagination) POST /api/projects/{id}/tasks - Create a new task GET /api/tasks/{id} - Get task detail with comments PATCH /api/tasks/{id} - Update task fields DELETE /api/tasks/{id} - Delete task and comments

GET /api/tasks/{id}/comments - List comments for a task POST /api/tasks/{id}/comments - Add a comment to a task PATCH /api/comments/{id} - Edit your own comment DELETE /api/comments/{id} - Delete a comment

ui_layout.structure:
Root layout: - Header bar (logo, project switcher, dark mode toggle, user avatar dropdown) - Left sidebar (desktop): Projects list with "+ New Project" button - Main content area (fills remaining space on desktop; full width on mobile) - Toast notification portal (top-right corner)
Pages: / - Redirect to /projects if logged in, else /login /login - Email + password login form, link to register /register - Email + display name + password registration form /projects - Cards grid of all joined projects /projects/[id] - Task board (Kanban columns: Todo, In Progress, Done) /projects/[id]/settings - Project name, description, members, danger zone /tasks/[id] - Task detail: description, assignee, due date, comments /settings - User profile, password change

design_system.color_palette: Primary: #6366F1 (indigo-500) — buttons, links, active states Primary dark: #4F46E5 (indigo-600) — hover states Success: #22C55E (green-500) — success toasts, completed tasks Warning: #F59E0B (amber-500) — overdue tasks, warnings Danger: #EF4444 (red-500) — delete buttons, error messages Neutral: Gray scale (slate-50 through slate-900) — backgrounds, text, borders Dark mode: slate-900 background, slate-100 text, indigo-400 primary
design_system.typography: Font family: Inter (Google Fonts) — clean, readable for data-dense UIs Headings: font-semibold, tracking-tight Body: text-sm (14px) for table data, text-base (16px) for forms
design_system.animations: Page transitions: 150ms fade Toast: slide-in from right, fade-out on dismiss Skeleton: shimmer animation (left-to-right gradient sweep) Modals: scale + fade in (200ms)

interaction.name: Create a project and invite a teammate

key_interactions.interaction: 1. User navigates to /projects and clicks "New Project" 2. User enters project name and optional description, clicks "Create" 3. System creates the project and redirects to /projects/{id} 4. User opens project settings and clicks "Invite member" 5. User enters the teammate's email and clicks "Send Invite" 6. System sends the invitation email; teammate accepts and joins the project

1. User opens a project and clicks "Add Task" 2. User enters the task title, sets the due date, and assigns it to a teammate 3. User clicks "Create Task" 4. System adds the task to the Todo column and shows a success toast 5. Assignee sees the new task appear on their board

interaction.name: Move a task to done and leave a comment

key_interactions.interaction: 1. User opens a task from the board 2. User changes status from "In Progress" to "Done" via the status dropdown 3. System updates the task and moves the card to the Done column 4. User types a comment ("Merged in PR #42") and clicks "Post Comment" 5. System saves the comment and displays it below the task description

implementation_steps.phase: Set up Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind project Set up FastAPI project with SQLAlchemy async, Alembic, and Pydantic v2 Configure PostgreSQL and run initial Alembic migration Implement users and refresh_tokens tables Implement POST /api/auth/register with email validation and bcrypt hashing Implement POST /api/auth/login returning JWT access and refresh tokens Implement POST /api/auth/logout and POST /api/auth/refresh Implement JWT middleware for protecting all /api/* routes except /api/auth/* Set up CORS and rate-limiting middleware on FastAPI Build /login and /register pages with inline form validation Build /settings page for profile update and password change Implement silent token refresh in the React API client Configure docker-compose with FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and Vite dev server

Implement projects, project_members, tasks tables and Alembic migration Implement GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/projects endpoints Implement project invite flow (POST /api/projects/{id}/invites) Implement GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/tasks endpoints with filtering and pagination Build /projects page: project cards grid with "New Project" button Build /projects/{id} Kanban board with Todo, In Progress, and Done columns Implement drag-to-move task cards between Kanban columns Build task detail page /tasks/{id} with editable title, description, assignee, due date, priority Implement overdue task highlighting (red border/badge) on the board and task list Build /projects/{id}/settings page: name, description, members list, danger zone Implement filter and sort controls on the task list (status, assignee, priority, due date) Preserve filter/sort state in URL query parameters

Implement comments table and Alembic migration Implement GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/comments endpoints with soft-delete Build comment thread on the task detail page with relative timestamps Implement dark mode toggle (system preference + localStorage override) Ensure full keyboard navigation and WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across all pages Add skeleton loading screens to projects list, task board, and task detail Implement toast notification system (success, error, info) Write pytest + httpx API integration tests for all happy paths and key error cases Write Playwright e2e tests for the three key user flows Conduct responsive design audit on 375px, 768px, and 1280px viewports

success_criteria.functionality: All feature bullets implemented and manually verified User can register, log in, create a project, invite a member, create tasks, and post comments end-to-end JWT refresh works silently without the user being logged out Project-level access control correctly rejects requests from non-members (returns 403) Task filtering and sorting return correct results and URL params reflect state
success_criteria.ux: App is fully usable on iPhone SE (375px) and up without horizontal scrolling All loading states use skeleton screens — no jarring blank flashes All destructive actions show a confirmation dialog before executing Dark mode works across all pages and persists after page refresh Keyboard navigation reaches all interactive elements without a mouse
success_criteria.technical_quality: All API endpoints return correct HTTP status codes and JSON envelopes JWT authentication protects all /api/* routes except /api/auth/* Database uses transactions for all multi-table writes No N+1 query issues in task list endpoint (eager load assignee in a single query) API integration test coverage: all happy paths and key error cases covered No TypeScript type errors (tsc --noEmit passes) No Python type errors (mypy passes with --strict) No secrets committed to source control (use .env files, documented in .env.example)
